title: Experiment 2
content: Hrs. 30 Min. Prerequisites (if any) None Course Type Lab Course Objectives: 1. To provide students with hands-on experience in inspecting, servicing on various electronics circuits associated with automobiles. 2. To equip students with the practical skills necessary for diagnosing, maintaining, and adjusting electronics associated with automobiles. 3. To enable students to proficiently perform and interpret diagnostic tests and measurements, using advanced technological systems for electronic systems. Expt. No. Experiments 1 Testing of various sensors used in automobiles. 2 Testing and validation of logic gate ICs using ARDUINO. 3 Testing and validation of flip flops using ARDUINO. 4 Testing of Diodes, Transistor, Op-amps, Relays in automotive electronic circuits. 5 Testing and validation of flip flops. 6 Programming and interfacing of Microcontrollers/Microprocessors 7 Interfacing of AC/DC converter, stepper motor and dc motor. 8 Advanced On-board diagnostics (OBD) and fault detection in electronics circuits. 9 Familiarization of engine remapping tools and software 10 Design and testing of the circuit for keyless entry, parking system. 11 Design and testing of the circuits for ABS, EBD and Traction Control. 12 Design and testing of circuit for electronic triggering equipment 13 MAT lab simulation for various electronic circuits (RLC circuit) 14 Study and testing of steer by wire technology. 15 Testing of NVH levels of an automobile. Note:- A Minimum of 12 Experiments is necessary. Course Assessment Method (CIE: 50 marks, ESE: 50 marks) Continuous Internal Evaluation Marks (CIE): Attendance Preparation/Pre-Lab Work experiments, Viva and Timely completion of Lab Reports / Record (Continuous Assessment) Internal Examination Total 5 25 20 50 End Semester Examination Marks (ESE): Procedure/ Preparatory work/Design/ Algorithm Conduct of experiment/ Execution of work/ troubleshooting/ Programming Result with valid inference/ Quality of Output Viva voce Record Total 10 15 10 10 5 50  Submission of Record: Students shall be allowed for the end semester examination only upon submitting the duly certified record.  Endorsement by External Examiner: The external examiner shall endorse the record 16 Emission testing of an automobile using sensors. 17 Testing and diagnostics of inverters used in electric vehicles. 18 Testing of regenerative braking systems in electric and hybrid vehicles. 19 Understanding and diagnosing Controller Area Network (CAN) bus systems used in modern vehicles. 20 Calibration and testing of systems such as adaptive cruise control, lane departure warning, and automatic emergency braking. 21 Testing and validation of electric vehicle powertrains including motors, controllers, and power electronics. 22 Understanding and testing vehicle telematics systems, including GPS, infotainment, and vehicle-to-everything (V2X) communication. Course Outcomes (COs) At the end of the course students should be able to: Course Outcome Bloom’s Knowledge Level (KL) CO1 Students will be able to recall the fundamental concepts and principles of various sensors and electronic components used in automobiles. K1 CO2 Students will identify and describe the functionality of logic gate ICs, flip flops, and microcontrollers/microprocessors used in automotive applications. K1 CO3 Students will understand the interfacing and programming techniques for microcontrollers/microprocessors and their applications in automotive systems. K2 CO4 Students will comprehend the operation and testing methods of advanced automotive systems like OBD, CAN bus, and engine remapping tools. K2 CO5 Students will be able to design, test, and validate automotive electronic circuits and systems, including those for keyless entry, ABS, regenerative braking, and vehicle telematics. K3 Note: K1- Remember, K2- Understand, K3- Apply, K4- Analyse, K5- Evaluate, K6- Create CO- PO Mapping (Mapping of Course Outcomes with Program Outcomes) 1: Slight (Low), 2: Moderate (Medium), 3: Substantial (High), -: No Correlation Text Books Sl. Rashid Pearson 4th Edition, 2013 2 Automotive Diagnostic Systems: Understanding OBD-I & OBD-II Keith McCord Motorbooks 1st Edition, 2011 3 MATLAB for Engineers Holly Moore Pearson 5th Edition, 2018 Video Links (NPTEL, SWAYAM…) Link ID Programming and interfacing of Microcontrollers/Microprocessors.; https://youtu.be/MqWeH3zp5GY Testing and validation of electric vehicle powertrains including motors, controllers, and power electronics. :  https://youtu.be/gDOnBwjF55U Continuous Assessment (25 Marks) 1. Preparation and Pre-Lab Work (7 Marks)  Pre-Lab Assignments: Assessment of pre-lab assignments or quizzes that test understanding of the upcoming experiment.  Understanding of Theory: Evaluation based on students’ preparation and understanding of the theoretical background related to the experiments. 2. Conduct of Experiments (7 Marks)  Procedure and Execution: Adherence to correct procedures, accurate execution of experiments, and following safety protocols.  Skill Proficiency: Proficiency in handling equipment, accuracy in observations, and troubleshooting skills during the experiments.  Teamwork: Collaboration and participation in group experiments. 3. Lab Reports and Record Keeping (6 Marks)  Quality of Reports: Clarity, completeness and accuracy of lab reports. Proper documentation of experiments, data analysis and conclusions.  Timely Submission: Adhering to deadlines for submitting lab reports/rough record and maintaining a well-organized fair record. 4. Viva Voce (5 Marks)  Oral Examination: Ability to explain the experiment, results and underlying principles during a viva voce session. Final Marks Averaging: The final marks for preparation, conduct of experiments, viva, and record are the average of all the specified experiments in the syllabus. Evaluation Pattern for End Semester Examination (50 Marks) 1. Procedure/Preliminary Work/Design/Algorithm (10 Marks)  Procedure Understanding and Description: Clarity in explaining the procedure and understanding each step involved.  Preliminary Work and Planning: Thoroughness in planning and organizing materials/equipment.  Algorithm Development: Correctness and efficiency of the algorithm related to the experiment.  Creativity and logic in algorithm or experimental design. 2. Conduct of Experiment/Execution of Work/Programming (15 Marks)  Setup and Execution: Proper setup and accurate execution of the experiment or programming task. 3. Result with Valid Inference/Quality of Output (10 Marks)  Accuracy of Results: Precision and correctness of the obtained results.  Analysis and Interpretation: Validity of inferences drawn from the experiment or quality of program output. 4. Viva Voce (10 Marks)  Ability to explain the experiment, procedure results and answer related questions  Proficiency in answering questions related to theoretical and practical aspects of the subject. 5. Record (5 Marks)  Completeness, clarity, and accuracy of the lab record submitted SEMESTER 7 AUTOMOBILE ENGINEERING SEMESTER S7 VEHICLE BODY ENGINEERING
